candidates.row_label: Depreciation in Cost of goods sold
candidates.why: Explicit COGS-related depreciation line, but it appears as a component beneath the selected parent row 'Costs of goods sold', so adding it separately would double count.
Operating amortization line appears in the COGS block and is part of the detailed build-up of the selected 'Costs of goods sold' total, not a separate same-level expense to add.
Appears within the COGS detail section and therefore looks embedded in the selected 'Costs of goods sold' total.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of intangibles excluding Goodwill in Cost of goods sold
candidates.why: Explicitly labelled as in Cost of goods sold, indicating it is a sub-component of the selected COGS row rather than a separate add-on.
This would be the preferred summary if populated, but it is blank in the excerpt and therefore not selectable.

variables.notes: Selected COGS reference bucket is the parent total 'Costs of goods sold'. The visible D&A lines sit inside that COGS section as components, so they are already embedded in the selected row and should not be added separately.

candidates.why: This is an SG&A-related depreciation line, but it appears as a component under the selected SG&A bucket headed by 'Selling and administrative expenses', so adding it separately would double count.
This is a component of the selected 'Administrative expenses' row and also sits within the broader selected SG&A area, so it is already included in the selected reference bucket.

variables.notes: The selected XSGA reference bucket already includes 'Selling and administrative expenses' and 'Administrative expenses'. The D&A rows shown for selling and administrative expenses are nested components of those selected rows, not separate same-level operating lines to add later. No R&D row is present, so no special R&D D&A adjustment applies.
